featured.title: CBS Evening News
featured.imageAlt: German flag in front of a historic building

content.opening: Working in the German healthcare system offers strong opportunities for international professionals. Germany has one of Europe's most developed healthcare ecosystems with stable demand for skilled workers.
content.sectionOneBody: Nursing professionals are needed across elderly care, hospitals, and pediatric services. Medical specialists, therapists, and technical healthcare staff also remain in high demand.
content.sectionTwoBody: Common roles include assistant doctors, clinical researchers, physiotherapists, occupational therapists, psychologists, medical laboratory specialists, and radiology staff.
content.sectionThreeBody: Most employers require formal healthcare education and verified qualification recognition before onboarding.
content.sectionFourBody: A German language level around B2 is generally expected for direct patient communication and daily team collaboration.

intro.title: The Bridge Between Talent and Germany
intro.descriptionOne: Welcome to Talent Seeker, your trusted platform for connecting talented professionals with opportunities in the German job market.
intro.descriptionTwo: We support both candidates and employers from first match to long-term success through relocation support and guided onboarding.

story.title: The Bridge Between Talent And Excellence
story.descriptionOne: We combine human support with practical hiring workflows to create strong matches between global professionals and German companies.
story.descriptionTwo: By aligning goals, skills, and market needs, we help both sides move forward with confidence and build lasting outcomes.

faq.title: Everything You Need to Know
faq.description: Can't find what you're looking for? Here are the most common questions about starting your career in Germany.

fees.question: Are there any hidden fees I need to pay as a job seeker ?
fees.answer: No, our services for candidates are completely free. We are compensated by our partner employers, not the talents.

language.question: Do I need to speak German before applying?
language.answer: Not always. It depends on the role. We guide you on language expectations and the right preparation path for each opportunity.

timeline.question: How long does the full relocation process usually take?
timeline.answer: Processing times vary by role and visa type, but most successful candidates complete the process in a few months with proper documents.

documents.question: Which documents should I prepare first?
documents.answer: Prepare your updated CV, passport, certificates, and work references. Our team reviews everything and gives you a clear checklist.
